Member.title: Member
Member.description: Represents a participant in the Chit Fund, including their personal details and contribution information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Member entity.

name.description: The full name of the chit fund member.

phone.description: The contact phone number of the member.

joinDate.description: The date when the member joined the chit fund.

monthlyAmount.description: The fixed monthly contribution amount required from the member.

status.description: The current status of the member in the chit fund (e.g., 'active', 'inactive').

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records a single monthly payment made by a member towards the chit fund.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

memberId.description: Reference to the Member who made this payment. (Relationship: Member 1:N Payment)

memberName.description: The name of the member for whom the payment was recorded (denormalized from Member for display).

month.description: The specific month and year for which the payment is made (e.g., 'January 2023').

amountPaid.description: The actual amount of money paid by the member for this monthly installment.

paymentDate.description: The date when this payment was recorded.

status.description: The current status of the payment (e.g., 'paid', 'pending').

ChitRound.title: ChitRound
ChitRound.description: Details of a completed chit fund round, including the winning member and the amount won.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChitRound entity.

roundNumber.description: The sequential number identifying this specific chit round.

winnerMemberId.description: Reference to the Member who won this chit round. (Relationship: Member 1:N ChitRound)

winnerName.description: The name of the member who won this chit round (denormalized from Member for display).

winningAmount.description: The amount of money received by the winner in this chit round.

date.description: The date when this chit round concluded.

definition.description: Collection used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). Each document's ID corresponds to a Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) that has administrator privileges. The document content can be minimal, often just a timestamp or empty object, as existence implies role. No specific schema is strictly required beyond existence, but we reference 'Member' as a placeholder to satisfy schema validation, though it represents a concept not a full Member entity. Authorization rules will check for the existence of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` to grant admin access across the application.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an administrator.

definition.description: Stores individual member details for the chit fund. Access is restricted to authenticated administrators only. Documents are self-contained, with `memberId` serving as the document ID.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chit fund member. This is the document ID.

definition.description: Records all monthly payments made by members. Each payment document includes `memberId` to link to the corresponding member and `memberName` denormalized for efficient display and querying without requiring additional lookups. Access is restricted to authenticated administrators only. `paymentId` serves as the document ID.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific payment record. This is the document ID.

definition.description: Stores details of completed chit fund rounds, including the winning member's ID and name (denormalized for display). Access is restricted to authenticated administrators only. `roundId` serves as the document ID.

params.name: roundId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chit fund round. This is the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for ChitFund Pro is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC), and secure Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) for an admin-centric application. 

**Authorization Independence:**
The primary authorization mechanism relies on the `roles_admin` collection. Instead of embedding authorization logic within data documents or using hierarchical lookups (`get()`), access to all core application data (`members`, `payments`, `chitRounds`) is determined by checking if the `request.auth.uid` exists in the `roles_admin` collection. This strategy completely eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, ensuring atomic operations and highly debuggable authorization. Any user attempting to read or write to `members`, `payments`, or `chitRounds` must first be verified as an admin via a simple `exists()` check against `roles_admin/{uid}`.

**Secure Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs):**
Each core data entity (`Member`, `Payment`, `ChitRound`) resides in a top-level collection (`/members`, `/payments`, `/chitRounds`). This structural segregation ensures that all documents within a given collection share the same security posture: full read/write access for authenticated administrators. This design inherently supports secure `list` operations, as administrators can query and retrieve all documents in these collections without requiring complex, performance-impacting filters within the security rules themselves. The rules simply grant access to an admin, and the application layer can then perform any necessary data filtering or aggregation.

**Denormalization:**
To optimize read performance and maintain authorization independence, denormalization is applied where necessary. For instance, `Payment` documents include `memberName` (from `Member`) and `ChitRound` documents include `winnerName` (from `Member`). While not directly for authorization in this admin-only context, this denormalization avoids costly cross-collection lookups for displaying related data, contributing to overall system efficiency and simpler query logic, indirectly supporting clearer security rules by reducing data dependencies.

**DBAC and Structural Segregation:**
Database-Backed Access Control is implemented via the dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection, which explicitly lists administrative users. This provides a clear, auditable, and easily manageable way to control who has administrative privileges. Each collection (e.g., `/members`, `/payments`) has a homogeneous security posture, meaning all documents within that collection are subject to the same set of security rules, further simplifying rule management and enhancing clarity.
